import {
  ColorScales,
  TypographySamples,
  RadiusSamples,
} from "./theme-demo";

export const metadata = { title: "Theme" };

# Theme & Design Tokens

One stylesheet defines Patch UI's color, type, radius, elevation, and motion values. Components consume those values through Tailwind utilities. A `.dark` class on `<html>` switches the runtime values without changing component code.

The system stays deliberately small: a neutral gray scale, two surface families, four ink tiers, four alpha hairlines, three semantic status roles, and one monochrome primary. Consumers add brand by redefining `--primary`; no accent family ships.

## Colors

<ColorScales />

**Base and layers.** `--base` is the application canvas (`#f5f5f5` light, `#101010` dark). `--layer-1` is the primary content and floating surface (`#ffffff` / `#191919`). `--layer-2` is grouped or selected content (`#f0f0f0` / `#242424`). Cards, previews, popups, Modal, Sheet, Tooltip, and Toast use this hierarchy. Form controls use fills instead.

**Fills.** `--fill-1` and `--fill-2` are neutral control and metadata fills. Input, Textarea, Select, secondary Button, Badge, Toggle, Kbd, and embedded actions start on a fill and step once on interaction. `--fill-1` intentionally shares a value with `--layer-2`, but the separate name preserves intent at the call site.

**Interaction overlays.** `--layer-hover` and `--layer-selected` are alpha overlays for transparent controls and rows. Hover uses the lighter overlay; press and selection use the stronger one. Popup triggers and pressed toggles use the held fill defined by their shared recipes. Alpha overlays remain visible on base, layers, fills, and consumer backgrounds without adding another solid surface rung.

**Ink.** `--ink` is primary text, `--ink-muted` is secondary text and the default icon tone, `--ink-subtle` is placeholder or missing-value copy, and `--ink-tertiary` is disabled or footnote text. Subtle and tertiary currently share a gray value, but their names preserve different semantic roles.

**Semantic status.** `--error`, `--warning`, and `--success` use fixed theme-invariant fills. Each role includes hover, active, foreground, and soft-treatment tokens.

**Hairlines.** Four alpha tiers keep boundaries consistent across surfaces. `--hairline-soft` is a whisper edge for quiet containment. `--hairline` is the standard floating boundary and divider. Stronger tiers provide structural or interactive emphasis. Most content surfaces and controls are borderless; hairlines are reserved for boundaries that clarify structure.

**Primary.** `--primary` resolves to `--ink` and `--on-primary` to `--base`. Hover and active values derive from `--primary`, so a consumer brand override produces matching interaction states. Focus remains independently monochrome.

## Typography

Inter is the body and display family. JetBrains Mono is reserved for code. Size and weight are separate axes.

**Size.** Eight utilities cover the system: `text-micro` (11), `text-mini` (12), `text-small` (14), `text-regular` (16), `text-large` (18), `text-title3` (20), `text-title2` (24), and `text-title1` (36).

**Weight.** Five utilities are available: `font-light` (300), `font-normal` (450), `font-medium` (500), `font-semibold` (600), and `font-bold` (700). Body defaults to 450 and headings default to 500.

**Line height.** Element defaults own line height. Body uses 1.5, paragraphs use 1.7, and headings use 1.25.

<TypographySamples />

Rules of thumb:

- Compose a size and weight, such as `text-small font-medium`.
- Do not add raw pixel type utilities. Extend the scale when a real role is missing.
- Reserve `font-mono` for code, terminal output, keyboard shortcuts, and values shown as code.

## Sizing and motion

**Radius.** Seven values are available: `--radius-4/6/8/10/12/16/full`.

<RadiusSamples />

Use radius 4 for very small controls, radius 6 for metadata, radius 8 for controls, radius 12 for content and floating surfaces, and radius 16 for large marketing chrome. Full radius is explicit and reserved for pills or intrinsically circular geometry.

**Shadows.** `--shadow-card` supports elevated Card and slider/switch thumbs. `--shadow-menu` supports lightweight popups and Toast. `--shadow-tooltip` supports Tooltip. `--shadow-modal` is reserved for Modal and Sheet.

**Motion.** `--duration-state` handles hover and press transitions. `--duration-overlay` handles opening and closing. Both use `--ease-standard` and collapse to zero under reduced motion.

**Focus.** `--focus-ring-color` is independent from `--primary`. Editable fields draw a crisp outline at the control edge. Non-editable controls use a compact keyboard-focus indicator and an appropriate state fill.

## Customizing

Override tokens after importing the stylesheet. Components update at runtime without a rebuild. Adjust the import path to the location of your CSS entry file:

```css
/* app/globals.css */
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "../styles/patch-ui-tokens.css";

:root {
  --primary: #8b5cf6;
  --on-primary: #ffffff;
}

.dark {
  --primary: #a78bfa;
  --on-primary: #0f0f10;
}
```

When overrides affect readable text or filled labels, run the [contrast checks](/docs/contrast) before shipping.
